kept.text:
Roblox has recently reached a settlement across 3 states, which are Nevada ($12m), Alabama ($12.2m), and West Virginia ($11.08m) amounting to $35.28 million. All states are requiring Roblox to introduce further safety measures.

Nevada's Requirements:
• Mandatory Age Verification for chat.
• Providing $10 million of fundings for "Boys & Girls Club and other nondigital activities".
• Provide $1 million to an online safety awareness campaign across 2 years.
• Lastly, provide a liaison that partners with Nevadan law enforcement agencies.

Alabama's Requirements:
• Mandatory Age Verification for chat.
• Communications amongst minors cannot be encrypted, allowing "enforcement to be able to more easily combat child exploitation networks, trafficking, and the distribution of illegal and harmful content."
• Provide a liaison position to coordinate with Alabamian state law enforcements.

West Virginia's Requirements:
• Mandatory Age Verification for chat.
• Alert minors when they enter a private chats so children "know how to communicate safely".
• "Dedicate a West Virginia-based internet safety specialist to coordinate directly with state law enforcement and conduct ongoing trainings."
• Provide $1.5 million for an public safety campaign across 3 years.
